close_reason: Added 'local' to mount options (was: fsname=turnkey,noappledouble,noapplexattr → now: fsname=turnkey,local,noappledouble,noapplexattr) so macFUSE volumes are tagged as local rather than network, avoiding Finder 'Shared' display and Spotlight network-indexing rules. All four options confirmed present in /usr/local/lib/libfuse3.4.dylib. Existing fuse_config setup (entry_timeout=300, attr_timeout=300, kernel_cache=1, auto_cache=1, negative_timeout=5 in fuse_init) is part of standard libfuse3.16 ABI and works with macFUSE — verified by passing fuse_operations layout tests. Deliberately deferred default_permissions / iosize= / daemon_timeout=: should be revisited after the 4vl.4 benchmark gives a baseline.
description: macFUSE has different/additional mount options vs FUSE-T: local (mark as local volume), negative_vncache, noreadahead, noubc, novncache, daemon_timeout, iosize, direct_io, default_permissions, sparse. Review which options benefit our use case. The noappledouble/noapplexattr options should still work. Consider: local (reduces NFS-like behavior), default_permissions (let kernel handle perms), iosize (tune I/O buffer). Also verify fuse_config fields (entry_timeout, attr_timeout, kernel_cache) work with macFUSE.
title: Update mount options for macFUSE
